items.raw_claim: Vercel Pro/Enterprise Functions can now run up to 30 minutes for Node.js and Python runtimes, up from the previous 800-second limit.
items.summary: Vercel Pro and Enterprise Functions (Node.js and Python runtimes) now support up to 30-minute execution durations, more than doubling the previous ~13-minute (800s) cap. Relevant for AI pipeline functions, batch processing, or document generation tasks that previously required a separate queue/worker architecture. Other runtimes coming soon.
items.title: Vercel Functions can now run up to 30 minutes
items.uncertainty: Pro/Enterprise only; other runtimes not yet supported.
items.verdict_reason: Meaningful platform limit increase for Vercel users; reduces need for separate worker infrastructure for long AI tasks.
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Changes the calculus for long-running tasks on Vercel — previously you'd need a queue/worker setup to stay under the old limit.

items.raw_claim: Cybersecurity expert Kate Moussouris argues that applying export controls to AI models due to the Fable 5 jailbreak harms US cyber defense capabilities.
items.summary: Simon Willison quotes Kate Moussouris (Luta Security) on the Fable 5 jailbreak situation: the White House classified Claude as a potential weapons-enabling tool, Anthropic is pushing back. Policy commentary with no independent actionable claim beyond the main model-offline story.
items.title: The Fable 5 Export Controls Harm US Cyber Defense
items.uncertainty: Policy situation is evolving; outcome unclear.
items.verdict_reason: Policy commentary; covered better by the main Anthropic offline story.
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Simon Willison quotes Kate Moussouris (Luta Security) on the Fable 5 jailbreak situation: the White House classified Claude as a potential weapons-enabling tool, Anthropic is pushing back. Policy commentary with no independent actionable claim beyond the main model-offline story.

items.raw_claim: Katie Moussouris confirmed the Fable jailbreak of Claude was real but argues that applying export controls as a result harms US cyber defense.
items.summary: Simon Willison quotes The Atlantic's Matteo Wong on the Fable jailbreak: cybersecurity expert Moussouris confirmed the jailbreak was genuine but argues export controls are the wrong response. Duplicate angle on the main Anthropic outage story.
items.title: Anthropic/Claude and the Fable 5 export control situation — The Atlantic
items.uncertainty: Attribution via anonymous sources; Anthropic not commenting publicly.
items.verdict_reason: Duplicate of the main Anthropic outage story; no additional actionable information.
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Simon Willison quotes The Atlantic's Matteo Wong on the Fable jailbreak: cybersecurity expert Moussouris confirmed the jailbreak was genuine but argues export controls are the wrong response. Duplicate angle on the main Anthropic outage story.

items.raw_claim: Cloudflare WAF Managed Challenge rules can trigger CAPTCHA on URLs containing literal ampersands, breaking faceted search engines and similar query-string-heavy pages.
items.summary: Simon Willison discovered that Cloudflare's WAF Managed Challenge rules can trigger a CAPTCHA on requests with literal ampersands (&) in the URL, breaking faceted search query strings. Workaround: URL-encode ampersands or scope WAF rules more tightly. Easy to miss; hard to debug without knowing the root cause.
items.title: Cloudflare CAPTCHA triggered by URLs containing ampersands
items.uncertainty: May depend on specific WAF rule configuration; not necessarily universal across all Cloudflare setups.
items.verdict_reason: Practical debugging tip for Cloudflare WAF users; saves significant debugging time.
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Practical gotcha that would be hard to debug without knowing the cause — saves hours of debugging time.

items.raw_claim: datasette-agent 0.3a0 adds execute_write_sql tool that requests user approval before writing to the database, implementing human-in-the-loop for agent DB writes.
items.summary: Simon Willison released datasette-agent 0.3a0, adding an `execute_write_sql` MCP tool that presents a write query to the user for approval before executing it against the database. This is a concrete working implementation of the 'dangerous action requires human confirmation' pattern for AI agent workflows — directly applicable to MCP server design.
items.title: datasette-agent 0.3a0: execute_write_sql with user approval
items.verdict_reason: Concrete agent workflow pattern (human approval for writes) from a credible practitioner; directly relevant to MCP work.
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Human approval pattern for agent DB writes is directly applicable to MCP server design for agentic workflows.

items.raw_claim: Anthropic's Claude models were taken offline due to conflict with the US government over the Fable 5 jailbreak and export control classification.
items.summary: An Axios investigation (via Simon Willison) reports that Anthropic's Claude models were taken offline after conflict with the White House over the 'Fable 5' jailbreak and export control designation. Sources close to Anthropic describe the shutdown as retaliatory, saying 'they screwed us.' For any developer running Claude-dependent workflows or agents, this is a concrete reminder that API availability is not guaranteed during policy disputes — worth having a multi-provider fallback strategy.
items.title: Anthropic's Claude models went offline due to US government policy clash over Fable jailbreak
items.uncertainty: Based on anonymous sources cited in Axios; Anthropic has not made official statements. Exact timeline and restoration status unclear.
items.verdict_reason: Direct impact on Claude API reliability; any builder dependent on Claude should know models can go offline due to policy disputes.
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Direct impact on Claude API availability — affects any builder running Claude-dependent automation or agents.

items.worth_mentioning_reason: Raymond Chen (Old New Thing) recounts a story from Microsoft's x86-on-ARM emulation team finding application code so broken they implemented runtime patches in the emulator itself. Historical tech story with no actionable implications for current projects.

items.raw_claim: A security researcher documented a supply chain attack where a LinkedIn job offer directed developers to clone a GitHub repo that installed a persistent backdoor.
items.summary: A security researcher documents a supply chain attack delivered via LinkedIn: an attacker sends a polished job offer with a GitHub repo to clone and run. The code installs a persistent backdoor while appearing to be a legitimate coding exercise. The attack passes casual code review. Any developer who evaluates contractor code, open-source contributions, or take-home tests should sandbox unknown repos.
items.title: A backdoor in a LinkedIn job offer
items.uncertainty: Specifics of the backdoor mechanism depend on reading the full post.
items.verdict_reason: Documented real-world attack targeting developers via LinkedIn job offers; directly actionable — sandbox unknown repos.
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Real documented attack vector targeting developers via LinkedIn — high risk for solo devs who review code for freelance or contract work.

items.raw_claim: Iroh 1.0 released — a stable Rust P2P networking library that uses public keys instead of IPs for connections, with NAT traversal and relay fallback.
items.summary: Iroh, a Rust-based peer-to-peer networking library, has reached v1.0. It enables direct encrypted connections between devices using public keys instead of IP addresses, with NAT traversal and relay fallback. API is now stable. Potentially useful for agent-to-agent networking or building tools that need device-to-device connections without centralized infrastructure.
items.title: Iroh 1.0: Dial Keys, not IPs
items.uncertainty: Rust-only for now; ecosystem adoption unclear.
items.verdict_reason: Notable v1.0 P2P library with stable API; worth tracking for agent networking use cases.
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Stable 1.0 P2P library for direct device-to-device connections without centralized infrastructure.

private_memo:
# Morning memo — 2026-06-17

**Source failures (if any):** None observed.

## Worth attention

- **Anthropic's Claude models went offline due to US government policy clash** — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/15/axios-clashes-anthropics/#atom-everything
  An Axios investigation (via Simon Willison) reports that Anthropic's Claude models were taken offline after a clash with the White House over the 'Fable 5' jailbreak and export control designation. Sources close to Anthropic say 'they screwed us.' For any developer running Claude-dependent workflows (including second-brain, multi-agent setups), this is a concrete reminder that API availability is not guaranteed during policy disputes. Consider having a multi-provider fallback.

- **A backdoor in a LinkedIn job offer** — https://roman.pt/posts/linkedin-backdoor/
  A security researcher documented a supply chain attack: a polished LinkedIn job offer directs the candidate to clone and run a GitHub repo that installs a persistent backdoor while appearing legitimate. The attack passes casual code review. Any solo developer who evaluates contractor code or does take-home tests should be aware of this vector and run unknown repos in sandboxes.

- **datasette-agent 0.3a0: execute_write_sql with user approval** — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/15/datasette-agent/#atom-everything
  Simon Willison's datasette-agent MCP gains an `execute_write_sql` tool that requests user approval before writing to the database. This is a concrete, working implementation of the 'dangerous action requires human confirmation' pattern for agent workflows — directly applicable if you're building MCP tools that touch databases.

- **Cloudflare CAPTCHA triggered by URLs containing ampersands** — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/16/captcha-on-at-least-one-ampersand/#atom-everything
  TIL from Simon Willison: Cloudflare WAF Managed Challenge rules can trigger CAPTCHA on requests with literal ampersands in the URL, breaking faceted search engines. Workaround: URL-encode ampersands or scope WAF rules more tightly. Easy to miss and hard to debug without knowing the cause.

- **Vercel Functions now support up to 30-minute execution** — https://vercel.com/changelog/vercel-functions-can-now-run-up-to-30-minutes
  Pro and Enterprise Vercel Functions (Node.js and Python) can now run up to 30 minutes, more than doubling the previous ~13-minute cap. Relevant for AI pipeline functions, document processing, or anything that previously required a separate queue/worker setup to handle the time limit.

- **Iroh 1.0: Dial Keys, not IPs** — https://www.iroh.computer/blog/v1
  Iroh, a Rust-based P2P networking library, hit v1.0. Uses public keys instead of IP addresses for connections, with NAT traversal and relay fallback. API is now stable. Worth tracking for agent-to-agent networking or building tools that need direct device connections without centralized infrastructure.

- **Typst 0.15 released** — https://typst.app/blog/2026/typst-0.15/
  Typst 0.15, the modern LaTeX alternative, released with significant new features. Worth checking if you do any technical document generation or templated PDF production.

## Full digest
- [P] [simonwillison] Anthropic's Claude models went offline due to government clash — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/15/axios-clashes-anthropics/ — API outage driven by Fable 5 jailbreak/export control conflict; plan for Claude API unavailability
- [P] [hacker-news] A backdoor in a LinkedIn job offer — https://roman.pt/posts/linkedin-backdoor/ — Supply chain attack via polished job offer repo; installs persistent backdoor on clone+run
- [P] [simonwillison] datasette-agent 0.3a0 — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/15/datasette-agent/ — execute_write_sql with user approval — human-in-the-loop pattern for agent DB writes
- [P] [simonwillison] Cloudflare CAPTCHA on URLs with ampersands — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/16/captcha-on-at-least-one-ampersand/ — WAF Managed Challenge triggers on & in URLs; fix: URL-encode or scope rules
- [P] [vercel-changelog] Vercel Functions now up to 30 min execution — https://vercel.com/changelog/vercel-functions-can-now-run-up-to-30-minutes — Pro/Enterprise Node.js/Python; 2x previous cap
- [M] [iroh] Iroh 1.0 — https://www.iroh.computer/blog/v1 — Stable v1.0 Rust P2P library; keys not IPs, NAT traversal
- [P] [hacker-news] Typst 0.15 — https://typst.app/blog/2026/typst-0.15/ — Major release of modern document typesetting tool
- [R] [gh-nextjs] v16.3.0-canary.52 — https://github.com/vercel/next.js/releases/tag/v16.3.0-canary.52 — Canary; rspack 2.0 + experimental Turbopack React compiler
- [R] [vercel-changelog] Workflow SDK inflight cancellation — https://vercel.com/changelog/workflow-sdk-now-supports-inflight-cancellation — Beta, Vercel-specific
- [R] [vercel-changelog] Workflow SDK TanStack Start — https://vercel.com/changelog/workflow-sdk-now-supports-tanstack-start — Too niche
- [R] [latent.space] AINews Satya on Loopcraft — https://www.latent.space/p/ainews-satya-on-loopcraft-building — Editorial newsletter, no actionable claim
- [R] [simonwillison] Fable 5 Export Controls Harm US Cyber Defense — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/16/fable-5-export-controls/ — Policy commentary; covered by the main outage story
- [R] [simonwillison] Quoting Matteo Wong / Atlantic on Fable — https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/16/matteo-wong-the-atlantic/ — Duplicate angle on outage story
- [R] [simonwillison] x86 emulator team found code so bad they fixed it during emulation — https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260615-00/?p=112419 — Historical tech story; not actionable
- [R] [simonwillison] John Carmack on Fabrice Bellard — https://twitter.com/ID_AA_Carmack/status/2064095424420487226 — Social media link, no content
- [R] [hacker-news] Banned Book Library in a WiFi Light Bulb — https://www.richardosgood.com/posts/banned-book-library/ — Creative hack; not actionable
- [R] [hacker-news] Garden of Flowers typography archive — https://garden-of-flowers.heikkilotvonen.com/ — Historical archive; not actionable
- [R] [hacker-news] FreeBSD 15 on a Laptop — https://www.sacredheartsc.com/blog/freebsd-15-on-a-laptop/ — OS report; not relevant to stack
- [R] [hacker-news] Iroh 1.0 HN discussion — https://www.iroh.computer/blog/v1 — Duplicate of Iroh 1.0 blog post
